Article Title: Hyderabad records its hottest temperature of season at 43.4°C; heatwave grips Telangana
Article Text:
:





HYDERABADThe city recorded its highest temperature of the season on Thursday, with several parts of the city crossing the 43 degrees Celsius mark for the first time this summer.The highest temperature in the city was recorded at 43.4 degrees Celsius in Karwan, followed by Chandrayangutta at 43.2 degrees Celsius and Amberpet at 43 degrees Celsius. Several other areas, including Uppal, Hayathnagar, Secunderabad and Kapra, recorded temperatures above 42.5 degrees Celsius.Heatwave conditions continued to grip Telangana , with 20 districts placed under red alert as temperatures remained significantly above normal across the state. According to weather department data, several districts recorded temperatures above 46 degrees Celsius, while heatwave conditions prevailed at 244 locations statewide.Peddapalli and Suryapet emerged as the hottest districts, recording 46.5 degrees Celsius each. Districts including Bhadradri Kothagudem, Hanumakonda, Jagtial, Jayashankar Bhupalpally, Karimnagar, Khammam, Kumuram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Mulugu, Nalgonda and Rajanna-Sircilla recorded 46.4 degrees Celsius.Mahabubabad, Nirmal, Nizamabad and Yadadri-Bhuvanagiri registered 46.3 degrees Celsius, while Adilabad and Warangal touched 46.2 degrees Celsius. Siddipet recorded 46.1 degrees Celsius, while Kamareddy and Jangaon registered 45.9 degrees Celsius and 45.5 degrees Celsius respectively.The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast that heatwave conditions are likely to persist over the next few days in districts including Kumuram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Karimnagar, Peddapalli, Jayashankar Bhupalpally, Mulugu, Khammam and Nalgonda.The department has advised people to avoid prolonged exposure to the sun, stay hydrated and take necessary precautions, especially during afternoon hours.
Article Summary: The highest temperature in the city was recorded at 43.4 degrees Celsius in Karwan. Peddapalli and Suryapet emerged as the hottest districts, recording 46.5 degrees Celsius each. Heatwave conditions are likely to persist over the next few days.

Article Title: Pep Guardiola responds as Manchester City consider naming new Etihad stand after him in incredible tribute
Article Text:
Pep Guardiola responds as Manchester City consider renaming new Etihad Stadium stand in his honour

Manchester City supporters have reportedly pushed for the North Stand to be named after Guardiola

Guardiola insists memories matter more than permanent tributes

Guardiola reflected emotionally on departures and final moments

Manchester City are preparing for life after Guardiola

Fans tribute to Pep Guardiola coach of Manchester City 1000th game during the English Premier League soccer match between Manchester City and Liverpool in Manchester, England, Sunday, Nov. 9, 2025. (AP Photo/Jon Super)

Pep Guardiola says the memories created during his decade at Manchester City matter far more to him than any permanent tribute, even as growing reports suggest the club are considering naming the newly expanded North Stand after him.The discussion has intensified following widespread reports that Guardiola is preparing to leave Manchester City this summer, bringing an end to one of the most successful managerial eras English football has ever witnessed.While there has still been no official confirmation from either Guardiola or Manchester City, reports emerging on Monday night claimed the Spaniard plans to step away a year before his current contract expires, despite publicly maintaining that he still has another season remaining on his deal.That growing expectation surrounding Guardiola’s departure has now triggered conversations about how Manchester City should honour the manager who transformed the club’s modern history.According to reporting from The Sun, discussions are already underway regarding the possibility of renaming the Etihad Stadium’s newly redeveloped North Stand after Guardiola.The newly expanded North Stand at Manchester City’s Etihad Stadium, which adds approximately 7,900 seats and increases the venue’s capacity to more than 60,000, is scheduled to officially open on Sunday during the club’s final home game of the season, adding further symbolism to the timing of the discussions.SunSport reported that naming the stand after Guardiola is considered increasingly likely behind the scenes as the club prepares for what could become an emotional farewell.Manchester City have already immortalised several modern club legends with statues outside the Etihad Stadium, including Sergio Agüero, Vincent Kompany, David Silva and Kevin De Bruyne.Guardiola’s impact on the club, however, extends beyond any individual player from the modern era.Since arriving at Manchester City in 2016, the 55-year-old has overseen one of the most dominant periods any English club has experienced, winning 20 major trophies across ten years in charge.His haul includes six Premier League titles, one UEFA Champions League trophy, three FA Cups, five League Cups, three Community Shields, one UEFA Super Cup and one FIFA Club World Cup.Beyond the silverware itself, Guardiola reshaped Manchester City’s tactical identity and helped establish the club as one of the defining football powers of the modern era.Despite speculation surrounding the North Stand tribute, Pep Guardiola appeared visibly uncomfortable focusing on personal honours when questioned ahead of Manchester City’s trip to Bournemouth, instead reflecting more emotionally on the lasting significance of the relationships and memories built during his years at the club rather than physical tributes attached to his name.“I have no idea about that! No, no, no. They don’t have to do anything, honestly,” Guardiola said.“The important thing in our lives is that when you look back you say, ‘Wow’ and with the biggest smile.“When you’re old and you’re a grandfather you say, ‘Ah my life’ and you can laugh about the memories. And that is the most important thing in life.”Even while speculation around his future intensified, Guardiola continued teasing reporters about the possibility of staying longer at Manchester City.“And whatever happens in this part of the season, and when I extend my Manchester City contract two more year, after that look back and say, ‘Ah how nice it has been!’“That’s the most important thing, by far. And I’m pretty sure most people we lived with in this time together, we can feel that.”Even while making those remarks, Guardiola continued publicly joking about potentially extending his contract, despite the increasing belief inside the club that his departure is now approaching quickly.when asked directly about what his own farewell could look like, Guardiola again insisted: “Next. I’ve said it so many times – I have one more year.”Part of Guardiola’s emotional tone throughout the press conference also centred around other expected departures within the Manchester City squad.He specifically referenced Bernardo Silva and John Stones, both of whom are also expected to leave the club this summer.“Bernardo can feel that and John can feel it. We spoke about last days and said, ‘What a time we’ve lived,’” Guardiola said.“When you are an old, old grandfather and you look back and see how your life was, you can laugh for the memories.“That is the most important thing in life. And we can do that, whatever happens in this part of the season.”Guardiola also briefly acknowledged that Bournemouth manager Andoni Iraola would be managing at the Vitality Stadium for the final time before his own expected departure this summer.“There are always last games for many people,” Guardiola said.“I’m pretty sure that Andoni will get the appreciation for the job he has done.”According to current reports, Guardiola’s exit could become official shortly after Manchester City’s final home fixture against Aston Villa.The club are also reportedly planning a major open-top bus parade on Monday to celebrate Guardiola’s achievements and Manchester City’s domestic success across the decade.The planned route is expected to begin in Manchester’s Northern Quarter before concluding outside the Colin Bell Stand reception area at the Etihad Stadium.Meanwhile, former Guardiola assistant and ex-Chelsea manager Enzo Maresca has already emerged as one of the leading candidates to replace him if the departure is formally confirmed.Whether the North Stand ultimately carries Guardiola’s name or not, the wider discussion itself reflects the scale of his influence at Manchester City.Very few managers leave behind an era so dominant that supporters begin debating permanent architectural tributes while they are still technically in the job.
Article Summary: Pep Guardiola insists memories matter more than permanent tributes. Manchester City considering renaming new Etihad Stadium stand in his honour. Growing reports suggest the club are considering naming the newly expanded North Stand after him. The discussion has intensified following widespread reports that Guardiola is preparing to leave Manchester City this summer.

Article Title: Virat Kohli 58 runs away from historic IPL record; set to overtake Chris Gayle, David Warner and KL Rahul
Article Text:
RCB's Virat Kohli (Image: X)

Poll Who do you think has a better chance of winning the match - RCB or SRH? Royal Challengers Bengaluru Sunrisers Hyderabad

600-plus aggregates in consecutive IPL editions:



3 - Chris Gayle (2011, 2012, 2013)

3 - David Warner (2016, 2017, 2019)

3 - KL Rahul (2020, 2021, 2022)

3 - Virat Kohli (2023, 2024, 2025)

2 - Shubman Gill (2025, 2026)

2 - Sai Sudharsan (2025, 2026)

Royal Challengers Bengaluru stalwart Virat Kohli is chasing yet another major milestone in IPL 2026 as RCB prepare to take on Sunrisers Hyderabad in their final league-stage match before the playoffs. The star batter is now on the verge of setting a new benchmark surpassing a historic record held by Chris Gayle, David Warner and KL Rahul.Sunrisers Hyderabad host table-toppers Royal Challengers Bengaluru in a high-stakes Match 67 clash tonight at the Rajiv Gandhi International Cricket Stadium, with a direct ticket to a top-two finish on the line. Both teams have already qualified for the IPL 2026 playoffs, but the contest carries huge importance as the winner strengthens its chances of securing the valuable double-chance advantage in the playoffs alongside Gujarat Titans.The two teams have faced each other 27 times in IPL history, with SRH holding a narrow edge with 14 wins compared to RCB’s 12 victories. Earlier this season, RCB defeated a Pat Cummins-less SRH by six wickets in the tournament opener on March 28, comfortably chasing down 201 with 26 balls remaining.Kohli is currently sixth in the IPL 2026 Orange Cap standings with 542 runs in 13 innings at an impressive average of 54.20 and a strike rate of 164.74.This also marks his fourth consecutive IPL season with 500-plus runs and the ninth overall in his IPL career.Now, the RCB icon stands just 58 runs away from creating history. Kohli is currently tied with Chris Gayle, David Warner and KL Rahul for the most consecutive IPL seasons with 600-plus runs. Gayle achieved the feat in 2011, 2012, and 2013, David Warner achieved it in 2016,2017 and 2019(He was unavailable in 2018) and KL Rahul scored in 2020, 2021 and 2022, while Kohli matched the record with 600-plus campaigns in 2023, 2024, and 2025.If Kohli scores 58 or more runs against SRH, he will become the first batter in IPL history to register four consecutive seasons with 600-plus runs.Even if Virat fails to score 58 runs against SRH, he has got opportunities in Playoffs to set a new benchmark and record in his tally.
Article Summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru take on Sunrisers Hyderabad. Match is final league- stage match before IPL 2026 playoffs. Both teams have already qualified for the playoffs. Virat Kohli is just 58 runs away from setting a new IPL record.

Article Title: “Three-against-one situation”: El Rubius opens up on being “targeted” in MrBeast’s viral $1M challenge
Article Text:
El Rubius reacted honestly after his elimination from MrBeast’s $1 million challenge, calling it “unfair” and claiming he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings. His response felt real and balanced, especially after MrBeast’s team later sent him a cake, softening the moment.

El Rubius reflects on a tough and “unfair” exit







El Rubius did not just walk away from MrBeast’s $1 million streamer challenge. He opened up. Soon after his elimination, the Spanish creator shared a raw reaction with fans, giving them a closer look at what the experience felt like from inside the game. It was not polished or carefully framed. It felt real, and that is exactly why it caught attention so quickly.The clip and his response have since fueled conversations across the streaming space. Some viewers believe intense competitions like this naturally lead to uneven situations. Others feel Rubius may have had a point about being singled out. Either way, his words have added a human layer to a challenge that many only saw as entertainment.Speaking through an Instagram Story, Rubius did not hide his frustration, but he also made sure to thank his supporters. He said, "Guys, I just got knocked out of the game. It was a bit of an unfair elimination, but hey, at least I managed to walk away with $50,000 for you all, which I’ve already distributed. It went to the mods, the subs, and the non-subs alike."He further added, "I’m happy, of course, though admittedly, it was incredibly tough.I wasn't really a fan of the challenge itself, either. But then again... it was basically a three-against-one situation the entire time. I mean, they were targeting me specifically the whole way through. I kept trying to go after RaKai, but, well, he actually just got eliminated in the end. So, at least justice was served for Abraham. Abraham, if you’re watching this, we were all rooting for you, and standing right there with you."His message carried mixed emotions. There was disappointment, yes, but also pride in how he handled things. Giving away his $50,000 winnings to his community stood out as a gesture that meant more than the result itself.He continued with another honest note, saying, "And in the end, karma finally caught up with that little punk. Sorry, I’m being a bit toxic right now, but let's just hope the MrBeast team doesn't see this! Guys, thank you all so much. Seriously, thank you for all the support and for just being there."He added, "Every time I asked for something in the chat or whatever it was, you guys were right there. You’re incredible. I’m a little disappointed in myself, personally, mostly for not performing better, but hey, I gave it my all, and we still walked away with $50,000, so we’ve got to look on the bright side. All right, guys, big hugs to all my brothers out there!"Interestingly, the story did not end with frustration. Later that day, Rubius revealed that MrBeast’s team had sent him a cake after his exit. It was a small gesture, but it shifted the mood. It showed that even in a high-pressure challenge, there is still room for respect.In the end, Rubius’ reaction felt honest and grounded. That is what stayed with people.
Article Summary: El Rubius was eliminated from MrBeast's $1 million streamer challenge. He called it "unfair" and said he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings.

Article Title: Ipl Ticket Scam: IPL craze costs techie Rs 1.46 lakh in fake RCB vs CSK ticket scam
Article Text:
Image credit: AI generated pic

Watch Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy

RCB register their second win

Poll Do you think the demand for IPL tickets is too high every season? Yes, it's excessive. No, it's just right.

NEW DELHI: IPL comes and the demand for tickets and passes go soaring up by the fans. The desperation to get inside the stadium to watch their favourite teams in action in the IPL go high and high. When the tickets get sold out, the demand gets too hig, and the cricket fans even ask for tickets in black. But for a 25-year-old techie the craze for IPL tickets for RCB vs CSK turned too costly and he was duped for a massive Rs. 1.46 lakh.The techie, a resident of B Narayanapura, fell into a trap by a post posted by a fraudster who posed himself as a ticket seller on Instagram.As per a report in Deccan Herald, the techie alleged in a complaint to Mahadevapura Police that an individual claiming to be Sumit Biswal promised he could arrange IPL tickets along with food coupons.As per the techie, Biswal was introduced him as a senior supervisor for ticket counter at M Chinnaswamy Stadium"He asked me to come near the stadium gate number 10, saying someone would deliver the tickets within minutes, and even sent an email confirmation to gain my trust," the complainant told Deccan Herald."Initially, I agreed to buy two tickets for Rs 3,700 each,but he kept asking for more money under various pretexts such as refundable security deposits, additional ID cards and food coupons.Trusting him, I made multiple payments - even using my mother’s bank account after exhausting my own limits—and ended up transferring around Rs 1.46 lakh," he added.Bhuvneshwar Kumar’s incisive spell of 3-41, coming after a marauding batting show from Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal, propelled Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) to a commanding 43-run victory over Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday.The contest saw RCB’s batting unit dismantle CSK’s attack with relentless hitting, as David’s 70 not out off just 25 balls and Patidar’s unbeaten 48 off only 19 balls provided for the late fireworks after Padikkal’s fluent 50 off 29 balls had set the tone for the daunting total.Their combined effort lifted RCB to a daunting 250/3, thus setting a new record for highest total in IPL 2026. In reply, CSK faltered under scoreboard pressure, with Sarfaraz Khan’s 50 off 24 balls and Prashant Veer’s 43 the lone acts of resistance amid a string of failures from the top order.Bhuvneshwar was at his disciplined best and even crossed the 200-mark in terms of wickets in IPL, with others also chipping in as CSK were bowled out for 207 in 19.4 overs. The comprehensive win also meant RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL’s history.
Article Summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) beat Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday. Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal put on a marauding batting show to lift RCB to a daunting 250/3. RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL's history.

Article Title: Clavicular Viral Controversy: "Trans women are.....": Clavicular’s viral moment with trans women sparks fresh conversation on internet culture
Article Text:
A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Instead of conflict, the exchange highlights shared influence and mutual awareness. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration rather than ownership.

Clavicular and trans women share a surprising realization about online trends







The video of Clavicular, a short-video producer, is going around the Internet, yet not the reason why. There is no yelling or over-the-top drama. It is a mere, everyday talk that seems almost novel nowadays, in the age of internet, which is overflowing with content. The scene is easy-going, the tone of the narrative is straightforward, and the characters appear more concerned with conversation than combat.The message that is being addressed is the flow of the conversation. It discusses internet culture, the way people communicate with one another and the place of trends. Rather than bringing out a debate, the encounter towards curiosity. It quietly brings attention to something that a lot of people who use the internet notice but don't often say out loud. People in different communities often have a bigger impact on each other than they think.The conversation begins with a confident and lighthearted comment from one of the trans women present. She says, "You know that trans women are like the OG lip masters, right? We are very talented in the program. Like, I do the peptides, I'm on Zeta." Her tone carries a mix of pride and humor, setting a relaxed vibe at the table.Clavicular responds in a way that keeps the mood steady but adds a different angle.He says, "That is funny because you guys were using a lot of the words in like the South Korean subreddits in like 2014." It’s not confrontational. If anything, it feels like an observation. He points to how certain words and ideas may have traveled across different online spaces over time.Another voice joins in and brings the moment full circle. "Guys, we're like the OGs. I saw a couple of posts about that. We're like copying each other." That line hits in a simple but honest way. It reflects something familiar. Online culture is rarely created in isolation. It grows through sharing, borrowing, and sometimes even unknowingly repeating.That's what makes the clip so powerful. It doesn't want to make a point or win an argument. It only shows a group of people talking, listening, and figuring something out together.This moment feels different in a space where viral content often leads to fights. It reminds people that conversations can be calm, thoughtful, and still important. And sometimes, that honest silence is what makes it work.
Article Summary: A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration.

Article Title: “A new chapter in my life”: New York Knicks centre makes brilliant move amid playoff battle against Cleveland Cavaliers
Article Text:
Mitchell Robinson. Iamge via: Adam Hagy/ NBAE via Getty Images

New York Knicks' Mitchell Robinson reveals his move to focus on the playoffs



Mitchell Robinson's performance in Game 1 against the Cavs



A new chapter in his life arrived at a tense moment for New York Knicks center Mitchell Robinson, who chose to step away from social media while the franchise pushes deeper into the playoffs against the Cleveland Cavaliers. Hours before Game 2 of the Eastern Conference Finals, Robinson shared a personal message that quickly caught the attention of Knicks fans already riding the emotions of New York’s dramatic postseason run.The timing was hard to ignore. Robinson has spent years being one of the Knicks’ emotional engines, bringing toughness, rebounding, and defensive energy whenever the game turns physical. Now, with New York chasing its first NBA Finals appearance in decades, the veteran big man appears determined to remove distractions and narrow his focus completely to basketball.Mitchell Robinson’s message carried both frustration and clarity as he explained why he was stepping away online.“Last post before I delete this app. I finally have changed my number for many reasons… as I fight through and keep fighting in this playoffs run 🤘🏾🤟🏾 my focus have to go to another level,” Robinson wrote before Game 2. “This is the start of a new chapter in my life 💚🔐 Love and will miss y’all…. Mitch out ✌🏾.”For longtime Knicks followers, the announcement felt significant. Robinson has never hidden from public attention. Over the years, he openly discussed mental health struggles, frustrations about his offensive role, and even lighter moments involving his trucks and personal life. At times, that openness also created problems.Earlier this postseason, Robinson and Dyson Daniels were fined following an altercation during the Knicks’ first-round series against the Atlanta Hawks. Robinson received a steeper $50,000 penalty after posting a vulgar Instagram story tied to the incident.Inside the Knicks locker room, though, the focus remains on surviving a demanding series against Cleveland. Head coach Mike Brown believes his experienced roster understands the urgency after escaping with a wild Game 1 comeback.“We have a veteran group,” Brown said. “I think our guys understand that we got away with one and we don’t want to put ourselves in that position again because Cleveland is a great team.”Robinson’s impact in Game 1 reflected both his strengths and the difficult decisions New York may continue facing in this series.In 14 minutes, he produced four points, six rebounds, and two steals while helping stabilize the Knicks defensively after a slow start. His activity around the rim gave New York the needed energy, especially during key stretches with the second unit.Still, Cleveland exposed a familiar weakness.Cavaliers coach Kenny Atkinson turned to the “Hack-a-Mitch” strategy during the third quarter, intentionally forcing Robinson to the free-throw line. The plan worked. Robinson converted only two of eight attempts, allowing Cleveland to maintain momentum.“I wanted to give him a chance,” Brown said afterward. “Mitch has been great for us the last few games in that situation. We’re gonna continue to give him a chance.”As New York stormed back from a 22-point fourth-quarter deficit in a stunning 115-104 overtime win, Robinson’s minutes disappeared late. The Knicks leaned into smaller lineups built for offense and spacing
Article Summary: New York Knicks center Mitchell Robinson announced he will step away from social media. Robinson shared a personal message before Game 2 of the Eastern Conference Finals. The Knicks are chasing their first NBA Finals appearance in decades. Robinson's impact in Game 1 reflected both his strengths and the difficult decisions New York may continue facing.

Article Title: Fans say Conor McGregor looks ‘washed’ and ‘stiff’ in worrying comeback training footage
Article Text:
Conor McGregor. Image via: Getty Images

Fans focused heavily on McGregor’s movement and speed in the latest footage

You can watch the new training video of Conor McGregor posted on Reddit

.

McGregor has continued projecting confidence ahead of the Holloway rematch

McGregor vs Holloway UFC 329 comeback fight/ Image: X

Other UFC fighters have openly questioned whether McGregor should still be competing

Conor McGregor is edging closer to his long-awaited UFC return, but newly circulated training footage has only intensified concerns among fans who believe the former two-division champion no longer resembles the explosive fighter who once dominated the sport.The footage, which began spreading widely across Reddit, showed McGregor working on the pads during preparations for his scheduled rematch against Max Holloway at UFC 329 in Las Vegas on July 11.McGregor has not fought since July 2021, when he suffered a devastating leg break during the opening round of his trilogy fight against Dustin Poirier at UFC 264.Since then, repeated comeback attempts have stalled.A planned return against Michael Chandler in 2024 collapsed after McGregor withdrew because of what was reported as a toe injury, while retirement speculation continued growing around the Irishman during his extended absence from the octagon.The latest clips immediately triggered debate because many viewers felt McGregor looked unusually heavy, stiff and lacking the fluid movement that once defined his striking.The Irishman appears to have bulked up considerably again, returning to a larger physical frame similar to the power-focused version of himself seen late in his UFC career.Several fans reacted critically underneath the viral clips.“He does not seem smooth at all bro,” one viewer wrote while predicting Holloway could stop him in the third round.“I see stiffness when I look in the McGregor division. They're stuck in the mud,” another added.One particularly blunt reaction read: “I think he needs the money, but he’s beyond washed. Literally nothing like himself now, he’s a thudding boxer with no speed or power lol.”The criticism reflects a growing uncertainty surrounding McGregor’s comeback after almost five full years away from active competition.Despite the criticism, McGregor himself has sounded highly motivated throughout the buildup to UFC 329.Earlier this year, he announced the Holloway rematch in typical fashion through social media, claiming he would “son” Holloway again more than 13 years after defeating him by unanimous decision during their first meeting at UFC Fight Night 26 in 2013.That original fight came before either man became a global UFC superstar.McGregor later went on to become the promotion’s first simultaneous two-division champion, compiling a professional record of 22 wins and six defeats, while Holloway built one of the strongest featherweight résumés in UFC history with 27 victories and nine losses.Ahead of training camp, McGregor also thanked fans publicly for supporting him during the most difficult stretch of his fighting career.The 37-year-old said the encouragement had given him renewed energy before the comeback, while also praising his coaches and sparring partners for helping sharpen his preparation.McGregor specifically mentioned longtime teammate Nicolo Soli, saying recent sparring sessions had pushed him to improve further.He also insisted he now feels mentally and physically stronger than ever before.“The support from fans has made me excited to begin an intense training camp,” McGregor wrote, while adding that he hopes his comeback can inspire younger fighters around the world.The growing criticism surrounding the footage also comes shortly after several UFC fighters publicly questioned whether McGregor should even return at this stage of his career.British featherweight contender Arnold Allen recently admitted he believes McGregor risks damaging his legacy by continuing to fight.“I don't think McGregor should come back. He's made his money,” Allen told SPORTbible ahead of his fight against Melquizael Costa on May 17.“The only thing he can do now is tar his career by staying too long. He's done everything.”Even with those concerns, McGregor’s comeback remains one of the biggest attractions in combat sports because of the scale of his name, the length of his absence and the uncertainty surrounding what version of him will finally enter the cage against Holloway.There is also already speculation surrounding what could follow if McGregor manages to defeat Holloway in July.One of the names repeatedly linked with a future blockbuster fight is reigning UFC middleweight champion Khamzat Chimaev, although much of that discussion still depends on whether McGregor can prove he remains competitive after years away from elite-level fighting.
Article Summary: Conor McGregor has not fought in the UFC since July 2021. The Irishman is preparing for a rematch against Max Holloway at UFC 329 in Las Vegas on July 11. McGregor's training footage has been widely circulated on Reddit. Fans have expressed concerns that the former two-division champion no longer resembles the explosive fighter he once was.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Greek philosopher Epicurus: “You don't develop courage by being happy in your relationships everyday. You develop it by…”
Article Text:
Epicurus (Image: Wikipedia)

Quote of the day by Greek philosopher Epicurus



“You don't develop courage by being happy in your relationships everyday. You develop it by surviving difficult times and challenging adversity.”



Know the meaning behind the quote by Epicurus



Why human beings naturally run away from adversity



Looking at Epicurus beyond common assumptions



Why relationships often become unexpected teachers



The strange way difficult times slowly reshape people



Other famous quotes by Epicurus



“Do not spoil what you have by desiring what you have not.”

“Nothing is enough for the man to whom enough is too little.”

“It is not what we have but what we enjoy that constitutes our abundance.”

“The greater the difficulty, the more glory in surmounting it.”

“Freedom is the greatest fruit of self-sufficiency.”

Why these words still matter in everyday life









There are some ideas that feel comforting immediately, and there are others that become meaningful only after life forces people to experience them personally. This quote connected with Epicurus belongs to the second category. It does not promise easy happiness or suggest that life becomes meaningful only during peaceful moments. Instead, it points toward something many people eventually realise through experience: comfort can make life pleasant, but struggle often changes people in deeper ways.Most individuals naturally move toward happiness. They want stable relationships, good days, supportive friendships and situations that make life feel secure. Nobody wakes up hoping for heartbreak, disappointment or emotional exhaustion. Nobody actively searches for difficult periods. People usually spend enormous amounts of time trying to avoid pain because pain itself feels uncomfortable and uncertain.Yet life does not always cooperate with those plans. Relationships face challenges, expectations break apart, people leave, mistakes happen, and circumstances change without warning.During such moments, most people focus only on surviving the difficulty itself because they cannot immediately see anything valuable hidden inside the experience. Looking back years later, however, many realise that difficult periods often changed them in ways that easy periods never did.Perhaps that is what makes this quote interesting. Epicurus appears to suggest that courage is not born inside comfort. Courage usually appears after people walk through something difficult and discover they are still standing.The quote seems to separate happiness from courage, not because happiness lacks value, but because happiness and growth sometimes teach different things. Being happy every day in relationships can create comfort and emotional security, which are important parts of life. But comfort itself rarely demands much effort from people. When things are going well, individuals simply continue moving through life without being pushed to discover hidden strengths.Difficult situations create entirely different conditions. During challenging periods, people are often forced to face emotions they would rather avoid. They may deal with disappointment, uncertainty, rejection or fear. Those moments are uncomfortable because they remove predictability and force people into unfamiliar emotional territory.Epicurus appears to be suggesting that courage does not suddenly arrive like a gift. It develops gradually. People build courage through experiences that ask something difficult of them. Someone who survives emotional pain may discover resilience they never noticed before. Someone who moves through failure may realise they can begin again even after losing confidence.The interesting part is that people usually do not notice these changes while they are happening. Growth often becomes visible only later.People generally associate difficult experiences with loss. Someone loses peace of mind, certainty or emotional balance. Because of that, the first reaction toward hardship is usually resistance. Human beings prefer situations they can control because control creates safety.This tendency appears in everyday life in small ways. People avoid uncomfortable conversations because they fear conflict. People delay difficult decisions because uncertainty feels stressful. Some remain in familiar situations simply because unfamiliar situations create anxiety.There is nothing unusual about this behaviour. It is deeply human.Yet avoiding every challenge creates another problem. People sometimes protect themselves so carefully that they accidentally avoid experiences capable of helping them grow. Strength develops through use in the same way muscles do. If something never faces resistance, it rarely becomes stronger.Many qualities people admire in others emerge through difficult circumstances rather than easy ones. Patience often develops after long periods of waiting. Wisdom often follows mistakes. Emotional maturity frequently appears after disappointment.Courage seems to work in similar ways.Epicurus is often remembered incorrectly as someone who simply encouraged pleasure and comfort. The misunderstanding probably comes from the modern use of words related to his philosophy. Many people imagine luxury, indulgence and endless enjoyment when hearing his name.His ideas were actually much more thoughtful and balanced.Epicurus believed happiness mattered, but he viewed happiness differently from endless excitement or material satisfaction. He often emphasised peace of mind, meaningful relationships and freedom from unnecessary fears. He spent considerable time asking questions about what truly creates fulfilment and what creates unnecessary suffering.That perspective makes this quote feel particularly interesting because it fits naturally into his broader thinking. He understood that a satisfying life was not built entirely from comfort. Human beings also need emotional resilience because uncertainty and hardship eventually arrive in every life.Relationships are usually where people discover many things about themselves. During peaceful times, relationships can feel effortless. Conversations flow naturally, disagreements remain small and emotional connection seems stable.Difficult periods create completely different conditions.Challenges inside relationships often reveal parts of people they never noticed before. Someone may discover they become impatient during stress. Someone else may realise they avoid difficult conversations entirely. Others may recognise strengths they never knew existed.Painful moments inside relationships rarely feel meaningful while they are happening. During those periods, people usually focus on fixing problems or simply surviving emotionally difficult situations. Much later, they sometimes realise those experiences changed them.Someone who experienced heartbreak may become more emotionally aware. Someone who experienced disappointment may become stronger. Someone who moved through conflict may learn patience and understanding.The process itself usually feels uncomfortable because growth rarely announces itself while it is happening.People often imagine courage as something dramatic. Movies and stories sometimes present courage as extraordinary moments involving huge decisions and obvious acts of bravery. Real life often looks very different.Courage frequently develops quietly.Someone wakes up after a painful week and continues working.Someone experiences failure and still tries again.Someone loses confidence and slowly rebuilds it.Someone experiences disappointment and continues moving forward despite uncertainty.These moments may not appear extraordinary from the outside. Most people may not even notice them. Yet these ordinary situations sometimes require enormous emotional effort.People rarely become courageous because life remains easy every day. They become courageous because difficult experiences forced them to continue even when continuing felt challenging.Some quotations remain memorable because they sound inspiring. Others survive because people eventually recognise parts of their own experiences inside them.This quote seems to belong to the second group. Most people eventually discover that the moments they feared most were not always empty periods of suffering. Sometimes those difficult periods quietly reshaped them without announcing it.Epicurus appears to suggest that courage is rarely born during perfect days when everything feels easy and predictable. Instead, courage often begins appearing during moments when life becomes uncertain, and people continue moving forward anyway.Many individuals only understand the truth while looking backwards. They realise that difficult experiences did not simply test them. Those experiences also introduced them to strengths they did not know they possessed before.
Article Summary: Quote of the day by Greek philosopher Epicurus: ‘You don't develop courage by being happy in your relationships everyday. You develop it by surviving difficult times and challenging adversity.’ People usually spend enormous amounts of time trying to avoid pain because pain itself feels uncomfortable.

Article Title: "She is going to end up in jail again": Asmongold fires back after Nina Lin blames him for spending night in jail over $4 scissors
Article Text:
Asmongold responded harshly after Nina Lin blamed him and his viewers for a situation that allegedly led to her spending a night in jail over unscanned $4 scissors. During a livestream, Nina suggested an Asmongold viewer reported her to police. Asmongold mocked her claims, called the incident “stupid,” and predicted she would “end up in jail again,” escalating the viral feud across Twitch and social media platforms.

Asmongold reacts after Nina Lin blames him for jail incident



Asmongold predicts more trouble for Nina Lin









A fresh online clash involving Asmongold and controversial content creator Nina Lin exploded across social media this week after Nina blamed the Twitch streamer for a bizarre incident that reportedly ended with her spending the night in jail. The drama quickly picked up traction online after clips from Nina’s livestream began circulating on X, pulling viewers into another chaotic chapter of internet creator feuds.According to Nina Lin, the incident started when she allegedly failed to scan a $4 pair of scissors at a self-checkout counter. She claimed the mistake was accidental, but the situation escalated after a store employee reportedly involved the police. During her stream, she unexpectedly connected the situation to Asmongold, accusing one of his viewers of targeting her.While explaining what happened to her audience, Nina Lin described the moment she realized the scissors had not been scanned properly.“I’m using the little red barcode scanner thing. But apparently I missed one... I have never been that stunned in my life. I was like, 'Are you serious? Are you sure?' I’m telling the officer: 'You see what I’m seeing, right?' And he goes: 'It was an honest mistake… but the guy wants you at the precinct.'”She then shifted the blame toward Asmongold’s community, adding, “Maybe he watches Osamagold... He's damn near a terrorist, that guy. Whatever that old white guy is.”The comments quickly reached Asmongold during one of his livestreams, and the veteran Twitch personality did not hold back while responding.“Osamagold?! I mean, my beard is getting pretty big, but is it that bad now? Jesus! My man! If this dude watches me, we got to VIP him in the chat. You packed this ... up. Sexual assault extraordinaire. Crash out queen. 638 subs. On the side of the street, malding about something that happened six months ago; you still remember it. But we know it wasn't an honest mistake; you're just stupid.”The situation escalated even further when Asmongold claimed this would not be the last controversy involving Nina Lin. During the same stream, he predicted she could land herself in trouble again while continuing to criticize her publicly.“Gotcha ... and it's going to get worse. She is going to end up in jail again, and when she gets put in jail, I'm going to make another video about it. This is going to happen again. And so, like, this is the middle of the story; it's not the end of the story. This is the middle of the story. So, keep this in mind, and you're going to keep getting farmed; you're going to keep getting laughed at. I'm going to keep farming hate on you, and it's because you're a loser and a . .. and you are just an awful person.”As of now, Nina Lin has not publicly responded to Asmongold’s latest remarks. Still, the exchange has already spread widely across Twitch, Kick, and X communities, with viewers heavily divided over how far both streamers took the argument.
Article Summary: Nina Lin blamed Twitch streamer Asmongold for a bizarre incident that reportedly ended with her spending the night in jail. During a livestream, Nina suggested an As Mongold viewer reported her to police. The veteran Twitch personality mocked her claims, called the incident “stupid,” and predicted she would “end up in jail again,’ escalating the viral feud.

Article Title: Who is Maurice Shelmon aka Ant Jefe? No Jumper YouTuber and podcaster faces felony murder charge after LAPD arrest
Article Text:
Maurice Shelmon, also known as Ant Jefe, was reportedly arrested by the LAPD on a felony murder charge, according to TMZ. The No Jumper podcaster and rapper became known through YouTube videos, music, and podcast appearances. Following the arrest news, No Jumper hosts reacted during a livestream and expressed shock while speaking about his recent online success and growing platform.







Maurice Shelmon, better known online as Ant Jefe, was reportedly arrested by the Los Angeles Police Department on May 19 in connection with a felony murder case. According to TMZ, Shelmon was taken into custody by the LAPD’s Robbery Homicide Division, and his bail was set at $1 million. The report quickly spread online because Ant Jefe had become a familiar face through his podcast appearances and YouTube content over the past few years.The arrest also surprised followers of the No Jumper podcast, where Ant Jefe appeared regularly. Along with podcasting, Shelmon built an audience through reaction videos, music releases, and interviews on social media. While authorities have not publicly shared many details about the reported case, several No Jumper hosts reacted during a livestream and spoke about the shock surrounding the news.Maurice Shelmon aka Ant Jefe built his online name through podcasts, music and YouTube videosMaurice Shelmon, 35, is known online as Ant Jefe. Besides appearing on No Jumper, he also worked as a rapper and content creator. He regularly uploaded reaction clips, podcast discussions, and music videos to YouTube. Some of his songs included “In a Benz,” “In the Ghetto,” and “Cali Dreaming.”During a 2024 interview on the Rich At Heart podcast, Ant Jefe explained how his YouTube journey started unexpectedly.He said, “Yeah, it was by accident. So, if you look as far as the profile, or when my channel was created, it was created in 2021 by my nephew.”He continued by explaining that he planned to focus on music after leaving federal prison. “I already knew when I got out, at the feds, I was gonna be on some music,” he said during the interview.Ant Jefe also shared that he did not begin actively using the channel until 2023, when he uploaded his first music video called “Unruly.” From there, his online audience started growing through music and podcast appearances.After news of the arrest became public, No Jumper hosts reacted during a livestream covered by USA Today. One host, Mooseman, said, “He’s like, Mr. I’m just a podcaster, YouTuber, I’m big chilling. So this was disheartening to see for sure.”Another host spoke about Ant Jefe’s recent success online and added, “He gave me a lot of advice on YouTube, showing me his YouTube earnings and how he’s been going crazy with that.”The livestream hosts also mentioned that they were unable to immediately confirm details about the reported charges through the court system. One speaker said the situation was “horrible to see” and described the arrest as unexpected because Ant Jefe had recently been speaking about improving his lifestyle and career.
Article Summary: Maurice Shelmon, better known online as Ant Jefe, was reportedly arrested by the Los Angeles Police Department on May 19 in connection with a felony murder case. According to TMZ, Shelmon was taken into custody by the LAPD’s Robbery Homicide Division, and his bail was set at $1 million. The No Jumper podcaster and rapper became known through YouTube videos, music, and podcast appearances.

Article Title: ‘Don't drop it,’ said Prince William as Novak Djokovic handed the 2022 Wimbledon trophy to Prince George
Article Text:
During a post-match meeting with champion Novak Djokovic, Prince George was offered the trophy, leading to a humorous moment as his father Prince William playfully warned him not to drop it. Image Credits: X/@Wimbledon (Screenshot)

The humorous meeting between a champion and a future sovereign on a balcony

Prince George experienced a memorable moment at Wimbledon's centenary celebration, holding the champion's trophy for the first time.

Royal tradition brought back online







Sitting eagerly in the front row of the iconic Royal Box, Prince George marked an important moment in his childhood life that was able to perfectly blend the elements of a royal responsibility with those of family norms. Being the first time for the young prince to make his presence felt in the famous Centre Court championship amid its spectacular celebration of the century mark, Prince George found himself literally in the centre of international news coverage. As everyone else in the packed arena watched the intense tennis play being carried out on the court, something special happened post-play.It is, however, almost four years since that amazing thing took place way back in the summer of 2022, but this will always remain one of the most memorable events among other royal lovers across the globe. With an elegant suit on himself, together with a club tie, he observed the whole thing with enthusiasm.Although the general public tends to assume that royals should always be in their formal best when it comes to important public events, in this particular instance, there were some real insights into what a genuinely good parent can actually look like in training their children for such moments. An unforgettable moment occurred during the typical post-match exchange on the secured club balcony, which brought an element of traditional family humour to a glorious British tradition.The most memorable part of the day came when the current champion, Novak Djokovic, appeared on the special terrace in order to meet the assembled members of the British royalty. Politely exchanging handshakes with the royal couple, Djokovic moved on to greet the young prince by asking him if he had liked seeing the tennis match played live, while suddenly offering to give him the heavy trophy. What happened next was a really funny family scene, which immediately won the sympathy of spectators and went viral on the Internet.As the giant gold trophy was put directly in his little hands, the face of Prince George changed at once from one of excitement to one of great seriousness, earning a burst of laughter from his parents. William Prince could not help adding a note of fatherly humour to the otherwise solemn occasion, telling his eldest son not to drop the priceless treasure.The light-hearted threat resulted in visibly shocking fear that swept across the young prince’s face as he tightened his grip and focused solely on the challenge of stabilising the huge trophy. Meanwhile, the Prince’s father struck up a conversation with the champion concerning strong serves, and Princess Kate slowly moved closer to her son with a watchful eye to ensure that the priceless trophy stayed intact until she took it from him.The priceless interaction between the family members was recorded and posted to Wimbledon’s official social media page , allowing the world a rare, unfiltered glimpse into the private lives of a royal family. The video shows a touching display of vulnerability exhibited by the young prince as he steadies the base of the giant trophy, all under watchful eyes.Throughout the discussion, Djokovic interacted positively with the young prince and even asked him what he thought of the competitive match, as well as some advice on how to handle such an important trophy. The playful mannerisms of the champion eased the tension that the young prince might have had at their meeting.As part of a beautiful reenactment of the actual award ceremony, which took place in court not long ago, Princess Kate delicately accepted the bulky golden trophy from her son. She returned it to the champion from Serbia. The light-hearted exchange marked the end of a truly unforgettable fortnight at the All-England Lawn Tennis Club, as people left with memories of a future king who is slowly learning the ways of his new job through childhood reluctance.
Article Summary: Prince George held the champion's trophy for the first time at Wimbledon. Post- match meeting with champion Novak Djokovic led to a humorous moment. Prince William playfully warned him not to drop the priceless treasure. The priceless interaction between the family members was recorded and posted to Wimbledon's official social media page.

Article Title: The night Princess Elizabeth slipped unnoticed into Britain’s VE Day celebrations
Article Text:
Britain’s Royal Family, including Princess Elizabeth and Winston Churchill, appear on the Buckingham Palace balcony during VE Day celebrations in London on May 8, 1945| Image Credit: PTI

A royal figure inside a public celebration

Why did the crowd not immediately recognise her

Britain’s Royal Family, including Princess Elizabeth and Winston Churchill, appear on the Buckingham Palace balcony during VE Day celebrations in London on May 8, 1945| Image Credit: PTI

A memory that became part of British history

Why the story still resonates







Among all the stories related to VE Day, perhaps none is as timeless as the night when Princess Elizabeth slipped into the jubilant crowds in London and was not recognised by anyone for some time. The charm of the event does not reside only in the fact that the young princess joined the celebrations. It also lies in the remarkably human element at the heart of the story: a teenage girl walking amid the crowds on one of the most emotional days in British history.VE Day on May 8, 1945, marked the end of the war in Europe, prompting celebrations across Britain after years of hardship and loss. According to reporting by The Guardian , Princess Elizabeth and her younger sister Princess Margaret were allowed to leave Buckingham Palace that evening and join the crowds outside under discreet supervision from royal staff and military officers.This is especially what made the night so memorable.What gives the story its lasting power is the reversal it represents. Instead of appearing as a distant royal figure framed by ceremony and security, Elizabeth appeared as part of the crowd itself. This was not a moment where the future monarch delivered a speech to the nation from a balcony. Instead, she was out on the streets of London, sharing in the joy of the nation.Reportedly, the future queen had joined others in the streets to sing, dance and move through the crowds that day. Reportedly, the event had eventually become one of the most memorable experiences associated with VE Day itself. That contrast between royal life and public life has helped this episode last so long. For a brief period of time, the divide between the two worlds was bridged. The future queen was no longer an observer of national history. She had become a part of it.An element of intrigue with regard to the incident is that Elizabeth and Margaret apparently managed to stay unrecognised for quite some time. Reportedly, it was stated that Kenneth Cohen, in an unpublished memoir, recalled being told by a family member to “look more closely” before realising the “two princesses” were nearby with scarves tied around their heads.Well, that’s an important detail because it makes the incident seem believable instead of something staged. The princesses were not hidden behind elaborate disguises. Rather, they were able to blend into the crowd, which was busy celebrating the end of the war and listening to music.The incident is also significant in terms of what it tells us about the spirit of VE Day celebrations. People were too preoccupied with the emotional significance of the victory to try to catch a glimpse of the royal family members. Isn’t that surprising?Over the years that followed, the tale slowly grew beyond the realm of royal legend and entered the broader cultural memory of VE Day within Britain. Accounts of the event on the 70th anniversary in 2015 often spoke of the "real story" of the princesses sneaking into the crowd and joining the celebration with other Londoners.Well, the story has all the ingredients of a compelling public narrative: a historical context, an assumed identity, an experience of liberation, and a final recognition. However, what makes the episode interesting is precisely its small-scale nature. It has nothing to do with constitutional history or royal protocol.The VE Day crowd story retains relevance because it allows a vast historical event to become small, personal, and relatable. Historical stories tend to be told in numbers, speeches and photographs. But it lives on, approaching history differently. It makes history small by focusing it on one moment in time: a scarf, a packed street, people singing together and a future monarch who moves through the crowd unnoticed.This story has endured because it rings true to both the spirit of VE Day and to the early public image of Elizabeth. Long before she became Queen Elizabeth II, she was a teenager standing inside a historic crowd, sharing in the relief of a country emerging from war.
Article Summary: Princess Elizabeth slipped into the jubilant crowds in London and was not recognised by anyone for some time. The story has endured because it rings true to both the spirit of VE Day and the image of Elizabeth before she became Queen Elizabeth II.

Article Title: AI storm engulfs Commonwealth fiction prize after winners accused of using bots
Article Text:
Jamir Nazir (Caribbean winner) and Sharon Aruparayil (Asia winner)







LONDON: For “The Serpent in the Grove”, another beast slithered in — AI plagiarism. Literary world erupted after a Commonwealth prize-winning short story set in rural Trinidad was accused online of being machine-written, triggering a fierce debate over literary authenticity and whether judges can still tell humans from bots.Trinidadian writer Jamir Nazir won Caribbean regional prize in Commonwealth Short Story Prize 2026 on May 14 for his work. Nazir, of East Indian heritage, won £2,500 for the story about a struggling cocoa farmer, examining endurance and marital silence through his young wife Sita, an orphaned village woman trapped in a loveless, suffocating marriage.Soon after, social media users ran the unpublished work through AI-detection tools and declared it machine-made. Some falsely claimed Nazir himself was AI-generated.Sir Lankan author Yudhanjaya Wijeratne wrote on X that Pangram Labs had flagged the story as “completely AI-generated”, adding: “I think Granta and the judges just got taken for a ride by Claude.” Caribbean judge Sharma Taylor had praised Nazir’s entry as “polished and confident”, adding that its “melodic voice” lingered “long after final line”.American author Daniel Friedman wrote on X that judges failed to recognise “all hallmarks of AI writing”, describing the story as rambling, clichéd and plotless.Ethan Mollick, an AI professor at Wharton, wrote on Bluesky: “In a Turing Test of sorts, it looks like a 100% AI generated story just won Commonwealth Prize for Caribbean region.”Online critics dug through Nazir’s LinkedIn and Facebook accounts, alleging his profile picture and posts were AI-generated because he promoted AI tools.The controversy quickly widened. Indian Sharon Aruparayil, 25, won Asia regional prize for “Mehendi Nights”, set in a Mumbai chawl. Her story also came under attack after British site UnHerd published an article titled “Invasion of Literary Bots”, alleging prose was “slop-ridden” and packed with semicolons — which critics described as another AI marker.Commonwealth Foundation, which administers the prize, said judges had deliberately avoided AI-detection software because entries were unpublished fiction. The foundation said none of five regional prizes were under review because shortlisted writers had twice declared no AI was used. After allegations surfaced, writers were contacted again and “reconfirmed their original answers”, it said.Foundation’s director-general Razmi Farook said uploading unpublished manuscripts into AI checkers raised concerns over “consent and artistic ownership”. He acknowledged AI-detection tools existed but said they were “not infallible”. Until reliable systems emerged, the foundation would continue operating “on principle of trust”, Farook said.Stories were published on British literary magazine Granta’s website, each carrying an editor’s note saying speculation over possible AI use was being taken seriously but stories would remain online “until definite evidence comes to light”.Publisher Sigrid Rausing said more than one entrant had faced allegations. She said Granta asked Claude AI whether Nazir’s story appeared machine-generated, and chatbot replied it was “almost certainly not produced unaided by a human”.“It may be that judges have now awarded a prize to an instance of AI plagiarism — we don’t yet know, and perhaps we will never know,” Rausing said.
Article Summary: Trinidadian writer Jamir Nazir won Caribbean regional prize in Commonwealth Short Story Prize 2026 on May 14. Indian Sharon Aruparayil, 25, won Asia regional prize for “Mehendi Nights’, set in a Mumbai chawl.

Article Title: 'Filter of fantasy': How anime is being explored as therapy for depression in Japan
Article Text:
File photo

Anime avatars instead of counselling

'It might help me get closer to the answer'







Growing up in rural Sicily, psychiatrist Francesco Panto often felt out of place, finding comfort and escape in anime, where he discovered characters who reflected the kind of man he aspired to become.Now living in Japan, Panto is trying to turn that personal experience into a new form of mental health support, using anime-inspired characters and avatars to help people open up about depression, anxiety and emotional struggles."The use of manga and anime supported me so much... they were very important emotional support kind of tools," Panto told AFP."Being raised in Italy, in Sicily, there were very strong stereotypes around gender or self-expression. But when I was 12 or 13 years old I started to play this game called ‘Final Fantasy’... and the male protagonists resonated with me," he said."They were so masculine and cool, but in their own way," he added.Panto recently completed a six-month pilot study at Yokohama City University exploring whether anime-style "character-based counselling" could make therapy more accessible, especially for young people hesitant to seek help.As part of the study, 20 participants aged between 18 and 29 who showed symptoms of depression received online counselling sessions from psychologists appearing as anime avatars with digitally altered voices.Panto believes the fantasy element can make people feel safer and less judged while discussing difficult emotions. He said the "filter of fantasy" may help people recognise and express their problems more comfortably.The researchers created six different anime-style characters inspired by familiar manga archetypes. Participants could choose whichever character they felt most connected to.One avatar was designed as a calm and dependable "maternal energy" figure carrying an assault rifle, while another was a "prince-like" male character wearing a cape and known for emotional sensitivity."I tried to infuse each character with a specific mental struggle. One character is called Kuroto Nagi. She's affected by bipolar personality traits," Panto explained.Other characters were built around themes such as anxiety, post-traumatic stress disorder and alcohol-related struggles. However, Panto said the goal was not to make the therapy feel clinical or overly heavy."The idea is for the avatars to be ‘fun’," he said, adding that psychologists were instructed not to make mental health issues too obvious during sessions.One participant in the trial, a 24-year-old anime fan and game developer, said they were drawn to the study after reading about a character described as "searching for true strength"."That made me feel like it might help me get closer to the answer to my own problems," the participant told AFP.Under the study’s rules, participants could not discuss details of the sessions themselves, but the young developer said anime had long been a source of emotional strength. "It gave me the will to live, seeing characters who are full of life as they work hard toward their dreams," the participant said.Panto is now also exploring whether artificial intelligence could eventually be integrated into the process, potentially allowing anime-style therapy without a real psychologist appearing on screen.The project comes as Japan continues to search for new ways to address growing mental health concerns, especially among young people struggling with social isolation and emotional stress.Mio Ishii, who helped lead the research project, said many people in Japan still face strong stigma around seeking counselling or psychiatric help."There are many young people who cannot go to school or continue working. So, our scope is to give them... new choices to recover from their difficulties," Ishii said, AFP reported."Because usually people have stigmas and psychological barriers to ask for help about their mental health," she said. "But anime or technology can decrease them," she added.The project also focuses on people experiencing "ikizurasa", a Japanese term describing those who find it difficult to survive or function comfortably in society.According to data cited by the World Economic Forum, only six percent of people in Japan had used psychological counselling for mental health problems as of 2022, significantly lower than rates seen in Europe and the United States. Experts outside the study also believe anime and fictional storytelling can help patients communicate emotions more freely.
Article Summary: Psychologist uses anime-inspired characters and avatars to help people open up about depression, anxiety and emotional struggles. Researchers created six different anime-style characters inspired by familiar manga archetypes. 20 participants aged between 18 and 29 who showed symptoms of depression received online counselling sessions from psychologists appearing as anime avatars.
